relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jesus passes through Jericho.
Zacchaeus is rich and chief among tax collectors.
He seeks to see Jesus but is blocked by crowd and stature.
He climbs a sycamore to see the passing one.
Jesus looks up and calls him down for today’s house-visit.
Zacchaeus receives him with joy.
The crowd grumbles over table fellowship with a misaligned man.
Zacchaeus answers grace with restitution and care for the poor.
Today salvation comes to this house and Abraham’s son is restored.
The Son of Humanity seeks and saves/heals the lost.
Near Jerusalem, expectation of immediate reign prompts a parable.
A noble person goes away to receive a reign and return.
Servants receive minas and responsibility.
Citizens refuse his reign.
Return brings account of what was entrusted.
The first servant’s mina bears ten more.
Trustworthiness in little becomes authority over much.
The second servant bears five.
Five cities answer fivefold fruitfulness.
Another servant preserves the mina in fear.
Fear imagines the lord as severe.
The servant is judged from his own mouth.
Even minimal faithful action was refused.
The unused mina moves to the fruitful one.
The bystanders object to abundance receiving more.
Having and not having reveal use or refusal of trust.
The parable closes with judgment on rejected reign.
Jesus continues upward to Jerusalem.
Near Olivet, two disciples are sent.
A colt not yet ridden waits to be loosed.
The Lord’s need explains the loosing.
The sent ones find it as spoken.
The loosing is questioned.
The Lord’s need answers again.
Garments become the humble throne.
The way is covered with garments.
Disciples praise the Divine for the powers they have seen.
The coming king is blessed with peace in heaven and glory above.
Some Pharisees want the disciples rebuked.
If disciples fall silent, stones will cry out.
Jesus sees the city and weeps.
The things of peace are hidden from Jerusalem’s eyes.
Days of surrounding pressure are foretold.
The city did not know the time of visitation.
Jesus enters the temple and disrupts selling.
The house of prayer has been made a cave of robbers.
Daily temple teaching intensifies opposition.
The leaders cannot act because the people cling to his word.
